column2: Hostname/device that emitted the log; scopes events and supports cross-host correlation.
Log channel/type label (e.g., audit/auth/dnsmasq/syslog); used for parser routing and source-level alignment.
Canonical event category inferred from the template; primary key for aligning semantically equivalent events across logs.
Outcome indicator when available (success/failure/unknown); supports outcome-based filtering and alerting alignment.
Primary actor entity when available (user/process/ip); used to align who initiated across logs.
Primary target entity when available (user/host/ip/resource); used to align what was acted on across logs.
Emitting application/service name (e.g., sshd, cron, dnsmasq); supports service/process-level correlation.
Process identifier when available; supports short-range correlation and linking events from the same process on a host.
Main object of the action when available (domain/file/URL/account/resource); enables object-centric correlation across events.
